raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.3,
    "R1.4": 1.0,
    "R1.5": 0.0,
    "R1.6": 0.0,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Faithfully reconstructing QuPPL's contribution claim — interpretation-uncertainty as a language-level primitive, calibrated abstain grounded in the two-axis decomposition — is prerequisite to any valid positioning verdict; a garbled claim renders the whole comparison moot.",
    "R1.2": "The three-block syllogism (each prior line lacks property X → no prior work covers QuPPL's point) must be internally consistent; if the blocks do not jointly exhaust the relevant design space, or if the synthesis overstates what the individual gaps establish, the positioning argument fails on logical grounds.",
    "R1.4": "This is the primary load-bearing task: verifying that the claimed deltas are accurate against the nearest prior work in each line (e.g., whether any universal PPL does expose an interpretation/world decomposition natively, whether question-under-discussion or RSA-style systems have been packaged as callable inference engines, whether selective-prediction work with structured uncertainty exists).",
    "R1.7": "Coverage of the three lines must be assessed for omissions that could undermine the novelty claim (e.g., neurosymbolic PPLs, probabilistic dialogue systems, structured-uncertainty abstention in NLU); characterizations of each line must not misrepresent the state of that literature."
  },
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": ""
}

rationale.R1.1: Faithfully reconstructing QuPPL's contribution claim — interpretation-uncertainty as a language-level primitive, calibrated abstain grounded in the two-axis decomposition — is prerequisite to any valid positioning verdict; a garbled claim renders the whole comparison moot.
rationale.R1.2: The three-block syllogism (each prior line lacks property X → no prior work covers QuPPL's point) must be internally consistent; if the blocks do not jointly exhaust the relevant design space, or if the synthesis overstates what the individual gaps establish, the positioning argument fails on logical grounds.
rationale.R1.4: This is the primary load-bearing task: verifying that the claimed deltas are accurate against the nearest prior work in each line (e.g., whether any universal PPL does expose an interpretation/world decomposition natively, whether question-under-discussion or RSA-style systems have been packaged as callable inference engines, whether selective-prediction work with structured uncertainty exists).
rationale.R1.7: Coverage of the three lines must be assessed for omissions that could undermine the novelty claim (e.g., neurosymbolic PPLs, probabilistic dialogue systems, structured-uncertainty abstention in NLU); characterizations of each line must not misrepresent the state of that literature.
